skill.name: pdf
skill.description: PDF processing skill for extracting text and tables, creating and modifying PDFs, splitting or merging documents, and filling PDF forms.
skill.summary: Process, analyze, create, and fill PDF documents with Python and command-line workflows.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mostly documentation examples, shell command references, license URLs, and keyword false positives. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. Risk remains medium because the skill intentionally runs local PDF helper scripts and writes user-specified output files.

medium_findings.title: Local File Read and Write Operations
medium_findings.description: Helper scripts accept local paths from command-line arguments, read PDFs or JSON files, and write PNG, JSON, or PDF outputs. This is expected for the skill, but it can overwrite user-selected paths and should be run on trusted working copies.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are direct and visible in the helper scripts. They appear legitimate for PDF processing, so the concern is controlled local file impact rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: External PDF Tool Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The documentation instructs the agent to run Python helper scripts and gives examples for command-line tools such as pdftotext, qpdf, pdftk, pdftoppm, and pdfimages. These are legitimate PDF workflows, but users should understand that the skill may execute local commands against document files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in documentation and mostly use fixed tool names with user-supplied file paths. This is a real operational risk but not evidence of command injection or malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted PDF Parsing Surface
medium_findings.description: The scripts parse user-provided PDFs with pypdf, pdf2image, and PIL. Malformed or very large PDFs could trigger parser vulnerabilities, resource exhaustion, or processing failures in the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly parse local PDFs supplied by the user. The risk depends on dependency versions and document trust level, so confidence is high for exposure but moderate for exploitability.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The reported URL findings point to license terms or explanatory references, not executable network requests. No evidence of PDF upload, telemetry, credential exfiltration, or runtime network access was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are visible in comments and license text. There are no network client calls at these locations.

low_findings.title: High-Risk Keyword Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and C2 keyword alerts align with ordinary PDF guidance, encryption examples, geometry tests, and documentation text. Review found no malicious cryptographic routine, command-and-control behavior, or obfuscation intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited files showed normal PDF processing examples and tests. The static labels do not match the semantic purpose of the code or documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command-Line Arguments Used as File Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: Several helper scripts take input and output paths directly from command-line arguments. This is normal for the skill, but users should avoid privileged directories and untrusted paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly assign sys.argv values to path variables and pass them into file-processing functions. The behavior is expected but should be treated as a local file safety concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local PDF Conversion and Rendering
dangerous_patterns.description: The conversion workflow renders supplied PDFs into images. Rendering untrusted or oversized PDFs may consume memory or invoke vulnerable parser code in dependencies.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The rendering path is explicit and central to the form workflow. The security impact depends on document trust and installed parser versions.

content.value_statement: PDF work often requires extraction, conversion, and form completion across many files. This skill provides practical workflows and helper scripts for local PDF processing.
actual_capabilities: Extract text, tables, metadata, and page information from PDF documents.
Create, merge, split, rotate, watermark, encrypt, and repair PDFs using documented Python and CLI workflows.
Convert PDF pages to PNG images for inspection and form layout analysis.
Inspect fillable PDF fields and generate field information for form completion.
Fill fillable PDF fields and add annotations to non-fillable PDF forms.
Create new PDF reports using reportlab and pdf-lib examples.
limitations: Requires local Python libraries and command-line PDF tools to be installed before use.
Works on local files and does not provide a hosted PDF processing service.
Scanned PDF text extraction requires separate OCR dependencies and quality checks.
Non-fillable form completion requires careful visual verification of bounding boxes.

use_cases.title: Complete Business Forms
use_cases.description: Identify PDF form fields, prepare values, and create a completed copy for review.

use_cases.title: Extract Document Data
use_cases.description: Pull text, tables, images, and metadata from reports for analysis or migration.

use_cases.title: Automate PDF Assembly
use_cases.description: Merge, split, rotate, watermark, or create PDF files as part of a document workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Extract PDF Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner text extraction from one document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the text from this PDF, preserve page order, and summarize any pages that appear scanned or unreadable.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze PDF Tables
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the tables in this PDF, extract them into readable tables, and note any rows or columns that need manual review.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a PDF Form
prompt_templates.scenario: Guided completion of a fillable or non-fillable form.
prompt_templates.prompt: Determine whether this PDF has fillable fields, map each requested value to the correct field, and create a completed copy for review.

prompt_templates.title: Build a PDF Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a repeatable local workflow that extracts data from these PDFs, merges selected pages, adds a watermark, and records any processing errors.

output_examples.input: A multi-page report with text, tables, and embedded charts.
output_examples.output: A page-by-page text summary, extracted tables, image extraction notes, and a list of pages needing manual review.

output_examples.input: A PDF application form and a list of values to enter.
output_examples.output: A completed PDF copy, a field mapping summary, and warnings for ambiguous or visually verified fields.

output_examples.input: Several PDF files that must be merged and rotated.
output_examples.output: A merged PDF with corrected page orientation and a concise processing log.

best_practices: Work on copies of source PDFs and write outputs to a dedicated folder.
Visually inspect generated images or completed forms before sending documents onward.
Install and pin PDF libraries in a controlled environment for repeatable results.
anti_patterns: Do not process PDFs from untrusted sources in a privileged environment.
Do not overwrite original PDFs unless the user explicitly requests it.
Do not assume OCR or form detection is accurate without human review.

faq.question: Can this skill fill PDF forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect fillable fields and can add annotations for non-fillable forms.

faq.question: Does it work with scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, but scanned PDFs require OCR tools such as pytesseract and careful quality checks.

faq.question: Does it send PDFs to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of network upload behavior was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Which tools does it use?
faq.answer: The guide references pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab, pdf2image, qpdf, poppler tools, and pdf-lib.

faq.question: Can it merge or split PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions include Python and command-line workflows for merging and splitting documents.

faq.question: What should users review before trusting output?
faq.answer: Users should review extracted tables, OCR text, field mappings, and completed form layouts.
